tweets.text:
How to create amazing images with Grok Imagine. 

You can also ask @Grok to generate image and video prompts.

quoted.text:
GROK IMAGINE PROMPT :
"highly stylized, hyper-realistic figure of a female warrior posed en pointe like a classical ballet dancer. She balances on the tip of her right foot, wearing pale pink satin ballet pointe shoes with ribbons wrapped tightly around her ankles and calves. Her left leg is lifted and bent slightly behind her, contributing to a dynamic, mid-motion pose.
Her upper body is clad in intricately engraved metallic armor reminiscent of ancient Greek empire. The breastplate is form-fitting, sculpted, and heavily ornamented with swirling relief patterns. Matching armored greaves cover her shins, and detailed bracers protect her forearms. The metal has a polished bronze or antique gold tone with elaborate filigree work throughout.
She holds a large, round shield in her left hand. The shield is richly decorated. In her right hand, raised high behind her head, she grips a short sword with a narrow blade.
Her helmet is covering most of her head and face, with a pronounced crest plume sweeping backward.
Contrasting with the heavy armor, she wears a soft, flowing, pale pink skirt made of sheer, gauzy fabric. The skirt flares outward as if caught mid-spin, reinforcing the ballet aesthetic and creating a striking juxtaposition between delicacy and martial strength.
The background is neutral and painterly, with muted gray-brown tones and soft lighting that highlights the metallic textures and the musculature of her legs. The lighting creates subtle shadows that enhance the three-dimensional, sculptural quality of the figure. Overall, the image blends classical warrior iconography with the grace and poise of ballet, creating a powerful fusion of strength and elegance."

I often add or remove details afterwards thanks to Grok Imagine's editing features.
For this image I made about 5 variations on the lighting, the background and I don't remember what other details.
@elonmusk I hope I have answered your question.

tweets.text: Starlink is a gamechanger

quoted.text:
Starlink Wi-Fi review on Qatar Airways:

"I used free @Starlink Wi-Fi on a 15-hour flight — and it just changed long-haul travel forever. I sent large attachments without delay, I downloaded documents, I responded to time-sensitive emails while crossing continents that I had neglected on our three-week venture through Asia. And the best part was that I didn’t once think about conserving bandwidth. The airline says speeds can reach up to 500 Mbps, and while I didn’t run a speed test mid-flight, the experience felt stable and fast, even over open ocean.

For remote workers, entrepreneurs, and anyone who doesn’t want to lose a full day in transit, reliable high-speed Wi-Fi at 35,000 feet changes the equation, and flying doesn’t disrupt your workload."

tweets.text: RT @BasedMikeLee: Excellent, comprehensive analysis of how the Senate can—and must—pass the SAVE America Act

retweet.text: Excellent, comprehensive analysis of how the Senate can—and must—pass the SAVE America Act

quoted.text:
In the SOTU, @POTUS called on the GOP controlled Senate to pass the SAVE America Act.  

Here is a primer of the Senate rules re how the GOP Senate can pass the SAVE America Act with a simple majority.   51 votes.  NOT 60. 

1.  @chiproytx and @BasedMikeLee, with support of @SpeakerJohnson put the entire content of the SAVE America Act into a Senate bill (S1383)  that had already passed the Senate and was at the HOUSE awaiting consideration. 

2.  The House passed S1383 containing the SAVE America Act language. 

3.  S1383 SAVE America Act is now back to the Senate, awaiting Senate action.  It can be called up and moved to floor by simple MAJORITY vote.  Not 60 votes.  Any time. 

4.  Once the bill is on the floor, it is ready for consideration. Sen Thune is in charge of it.  

5.  It faces a filibuster.  Thune can then do one of two things:
- try to cut off the filibuster which requires 60 votes and will kill the bill.  

WE DONT WANT HIM TO DO THAT.  That is a deliberate move to kill the bill. 
  - the other option is to simply allow the Democrats to filibuster the bill.  
THATS WHAT WE WANT

6.  There are 50 GOP Senators pledged to vote yes.  So if we can get to the other side of the filibuster, SAVE America Act can pass with 51 votes.  

7. Make the Democrats actually DO the filibuster.  Make them talk about the bill.

8.   Why do they oppose proof of citizenship for registering to vote?

9.  Why do they oppose photo idea to vote?

10. Why do they oppose requiring states to clean voter rolls?

11.  Let the filibuster run its course.  Let the toddlers have their tantrum 

12.  Then, when that’s over or when they are tired, the SAVE America Act can pass with a simple majority.  51 votes. 

THAT is what we want Sen Thune to do. It is all within his control. 

He just needs to stand up, look the Democrats in the eye and say “okay, guys, let’s go.  Let’s have a filibuster”

That’s what the American people want.  Don’t get sucked into the false narrative that the SAVE America Act must have 60 votes to pass.  Not true. 

What is required is a leader who’s not afraid to lead and a Republican majority who’s not afraid to work.  And to fight for something that matters to the American people.  

We are watching and pushing.  A cloture motion before any debate is intended to kill the bill.  Is that what Sen Thune wants- to be the one who kills the SAVE America Act?  
@EIwatchdogs
